name: renderbirdcore

metadata.name: RenderBirdCore
metadata.summary: A easy to use object-based 3D rendering engine for Python based on PyGame and OpenGL

documentation:
# RenderBirdCore

![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-0.1.0-blue.svg)

**RenderBirdCore** is an easy-to-use 3D rendering engine for Python based on PyGame and OpenGL. It provides a simple and intuitive interface for creating and rendering 3D objects, managing cameras, and integrating 2D UI elements, making it ideal for developers looking to easily build simple 3D applications or games with Python.

## Installation

You can install RenderBirdCore via pip:

```bash
pip install RenderBirdCore
```

## Usage

For detailed examples and usage guides, please refer to the [Examples](https://github.com/Wojtekb30/RenderBird-Python-3D-engine/tree/main/Examples) folder on the GitHub repository.

## Known Issues

- **Performance with Complex STL Models**: Custom STL 3D models may exhibit poor performance if they are highly complex or have large textures. It is recommended to use low-poly models until update to handle complex ones releases.

## Future Plans

Future updates for RenderBird include:

- **Enhanced 3D Model Handling**: Improved support and optimization for custom 3D models to handle complexity and textures more efficiently.
- **Performance Enhancements**: Ongoing optimizations to increase rendering speed and reduce computational overhead.
- **Lighting Support**: Introduction of various lighting options to create more realistic and visually appealing scenes.
- **Additional Features**: Implementation of new functions and tools to expand the engine's capabilities and provide developers with more flexibility.
- **Development software**: I plan to create programs that will make software development with RenderBird even easier.

## Why is it named RenderBirdCore?

I named it RenderBird because I like to add the word Bird to many of my projects. BirdEngine sounded generic so I came up with the name RenderBird. And this package is Core because it is core of the rendering engine, and other functionalities may release as separate packages using this one as the core.

## Community and Feedback

I'm excited to see what you've built with RenderBird! Feel free to share your projects and provide feedback to help improve the engine. Your contributions and ideas are highly appreciated.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.enter_fullscreen
documentation: Switch to fullscreen mode

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.exit_fullscreen
documentation: Switch to windowed mode

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.toggle_fullscreen
documentation: Toggle between fullscreen and windowed mode

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.enable_depth_testing
documentation:
Use value argument to turn it on or off.
With Depth Testing enabled, objects render in order as you see them. Objects closer to camera are rendered first.
Without it, an object that is behind another object may render like if it was in front of it, which may look confusing to say at least.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.GetPygameScreen
documentation: Returns PyGame screen object

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.run_once
documentation:
Run a function one time.
\:param function: Function
\:param args: The function's arguments
\:param remove_not_run: If True, it will remove the function from its memory, making it possible to run again.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.key_pressed
documentation:
Returns whenever a keyboard key was pressed.
\:param key_pygame_var: Key to check for, PyGame variable, for example K_w

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.update_display
documentation: Update the display to render things.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.set_background_color
documentation: Sets color of the background (or if you prefer sky)

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.clear_screen
documentation: Clear screen to get rid of old frame

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.safe_close
documentation: Closes the program ensuring PyGame and OpenGL stop too

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.handle_close_event
documentation: Checks if program is commanded to close by the OS (for example because someone clicked X to close its window)

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.handle_close_event_direct
documentation:
Checks if program is commanded to close by the OS (for example because someone clicked X to close its window)
Runs safe_close() instead of just changing running bool variable to False.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.draw_world_axes
documentation:
Draws the world coordinate axes for debugging purposes.

```
param length

Length of each axis line.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.set_perspective
documentation: Set up the perspective projection for 3D rendering.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.set_orthographic
documentation: Set up an orthographic projection for 2D rendering.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.reset_projection
documentation: Reset to the previous projection and modelview matrices.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.draw_rectangle_2d
documentation:
Draw a 2D rectangle using OpenGL primitives.

```
param rect

Tuple (x, y, width, height).

param color

Tuple (r, g, b, a) with values in [0,1].
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.draw_circle_2d
documentation:
Draw a filled 2D circle using OpenGL.

```
param circle

Instance of Circle_2D class.

param segments

Number of segments to approximate the circle.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.render_2d_objects
documentation:
Render 2D objects using OpenGL. Should be called after rendering 3D objects.

```
param object_list

List of 2D objects (Rectangle_2D, Circle_2D, Image_2D, etc.).
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Camera with position, rotation, and perspective settings.

```
param view_width

Width of the viewport.

param view_height

Height of the viewport.

param x

Initial X position.

param y

Initial Y position.

param z

Initial Z position.

param pitch

Initial pitch angle (rotation around X-axis).

param yaw

Initial yaw angle (rotation around Y-axis).

param roll

Initial roll angle (rotation around Z-axis).

param fov

Field of view angle.

param minimum_render_distance

Minimum render distance.

param maximum_render_distance

Maximum render distance.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.update_forward_vector
documentation: Updates the forward vector based on current pitch and yaw angles.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.rotate_pitch
documentation:
Rotates the camera's pitch.

```
param delta_pitch

The amount to change the pitch by.

param reverse

If True, reverses the pitch direction.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.rotate_yaw
documentation:
Rotates the camera's yaw.

```
param delta_yaw

The amount to change the yaw by.

param reverse

If True, reverses the yaw direction.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.rotate
documentation:
Rotates the camera by specified pitch, yaw, and roll angles.

```
param pitch

Change in pitch angle.

param yaw

Change in yaw angle.

param roll

Change in roll angle.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.setup_perspective
documentation: Sets up the perspective projection and camera view using gluLookAt.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.apply_rotation
documentation: This method is retained for compatibility but is no longer used since gluLookAt is employed.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.translate
documentation:
Translates the camera by the given amounts.

```
param dx

Translation along the X-axis.

param dy

Translation along the Y-axis.

param dz

Translation along the Z-axis.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.move
documentation:
Moves the camera based on input transformations, considering the current yaw angle.

```
param x_transform

Movement along the right vector (strafe).

param y_transform

Movement along the up vector.

param z_transform

Movement along the forward vector.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.look_around
documentation:
Adjusts the camera's yaw and pitch based on mouse movement.

```
param delta_x

Mouse movement in the X-direction.

param delta_y

Mouse movement in the Y-direction.

param sensitivity

Sensitivity factor for mouse movement.

param reverse_horiz

If True, reverses the horizontal look direction.

param reverse_vert

If True, reverses the vertical look direction.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.use_mouse_camera_controls
documentation:
Implements mouse-based camera controls by capturing mouse movement and adjusting the camera's orientation.

```
param window_size_x

Width of the window.

param window_size_y

Height of the window.

param sensitivity

Sensitivity factor for mouse movement.

param sensitivity_factor

Additional sensitivity scaling factor.

param reverse_horizontally

If True, reverses horizontal mouse movement.

param reverse_vertially

If True, reverses vertical mouse movement.

param mouse_cursor_visible

If True, makes the mouse cursor visible.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.get_world_position
documentation:
Returns the camera's position in world coordinates.

```
return

A list representing the camera's X, Y, Z position.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.detect_objects_in_view
documentation:
Detects objects within the camera's view based on distance and angle threshold.

```
param objects

A list of objects with a 'position' attribute.

param max_distance

Maximum distance to detect objects.

param angle_threshold

Maximum angle (in degrees) from the forward vector to consider.

return

A list of detected objects.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Camera.draw_forward_vector
documentation:
Draws a line representing the camera's forward vector for debugging purposes.

```
param length

The length of the forward vector line.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RunAfterTime.__init__
documentation:
---
param seconds: Seconds that must pass from initalization or last reset for the function
  to run.
---

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RunAfterTime.run_once
documentation:
Run a function one time.
\:param function: Function
\:param args: The function's arguments
\:param remove_not_run: If True, it will remove the function from its memory, making it possible to run again.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RunAfterTime.run_once_after_time
documentation:
Run a function once after certain amount of time
\:param function: Function
\:param args: The function's arguments

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RunAfterTime.run_after_time
documentation:
Run a function after certain amount of time, without lock to run it only once.
\:param function: Function
\:param args: The function's arguments

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RunAfterTime.reset_start_time
documentation: Resets the timestamp since which the function waits to run functions.
Allows to run a function after certain amount of time passes.
Utility and functions:

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.FPS_Limiter

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.FPS_Limiter.__init__
documentation:
---
param max_fps: Maximum frames per second, I recommend 30, 50 or 60
---

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.FPS_Limiter.code_start_point
documentation:
Put this at the beggining of your program's While (main)loop.
Reads time when it starts.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.FPS_Limiter.code_end_point_limit_framerate
documentation:
Put this at the end of your program's While (main)loop.
Waits until proper amount of time since code_start_point passed to ensure the top speed.
Allows to limit frames per second and program's speed in a consistent way, ensuring same top execution speed on all devices.

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Cube

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Cube.translate
documentation: Smoothly translates the cube in the 3D space.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Cube.rotate
documentation: Smoothly rotates the cube by specified angles.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Cube.update_rotation
documentation: Automatically rotates the cube using its rotation_speed.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Cube.draw
documentation: Draws the cube at its current position and with its current rotation.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Cube.check_collision
documentation:
Checks if this object collides with another object using AABB.
\:param other: Another object with a position and size/dimensions.
\:return: True if there is a collision, False otherwise.

3D cube object, left for compatibility.
Since you can create cubes with RectangularPrism, only it will be getting updates.

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism.__init__
documentation:
---
param color_sides: If False, use frame_color to set color of the frame. Otherwise,
  the other arguments will be used.
param depth: Depth
param height: Height
param position: Position in 3D space (for example (0,0,0))
param rotation: Initial rotation in 3D space (for example (0,0,0))
param width: Width
---

All color arguments expect a tuple with 4 numbers (Red, Green, Blue, Transparency).

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism.translate
documentation: Smoothly translates the prism in the 3D space.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism.rotate
documentation: Smoothly rotates the prism by specified angles.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism.update_rotation
documentation: Automatically rotates the prism using its rotation_speed.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism.draw
documentation: Draws the prism at its current position and with its current rotation.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.RectangularPrism.check_collision
documentation:
Checks if this object collides with another object using AABB.
\:param other: Another object with a position and size/dimensions.
\:return: True if there is a collision, False otherwise.
A 3D rectangular prism object.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.__init__
documentation:
Loads and renders a 3D model from an STL file with optional texture or plain color.
\:param stl_path: Path to the .STL 3D model file
\:param texture_path: Path to texture image file to use, set None for plain color.
\:param color: Plain color to use if there is no image texture used.
\:param position: X Y Z position in the world, tuple.
\:param scale: Scale of the rendered 3D model in %
\:param rotation: Tuple determining how to rotate the 3D model in degrees.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.load_model
documentation:
Parses the STL file and extracts vertices and faces.
Supports both ASCII and binary STL formats.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.normalize_vector
documentation: Normalize a 3D vector to unit length.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.generate_texture_coordinates
documentation:
Generates UV texture coordinates for the model.
Maps vertices to a normalized space using the bounding box.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.create_buffers
documentation: Creates Vertex Buffer Objects (VBOs) for efficient rendering.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.load_texture
documentation: Loads a texture image and binds it for OpenGL rendering.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.draw
documentation: Renders the STL model with texture or plain color.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.translate
documentation: Translates the model by the specified deltas.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.rotate
documentation: Rotates the model by the specified angles.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Model3D_STL.update_rotation
documentation: Applies continuous rotation based on current rotation angles.

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Rectangle_2D

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Rectangle_2D.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Rectangle with x, y coordinates, width, height, fill color,
and optional frame color and width.

```
param x

X-coordinate of the top-left corner.

param y

Y-coordinate of the top-left corner.

param width

Width of the rectangle.

param height

Height of the rectangle.

param fill_color

Tuple representing RGBA color (0-1 range).

param frame_color

Tuple representing RGBA frame color (0-1 range), optional.

param frame_width

Width of the frame line, optional.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Rectangle_2D.draw
documentation: Draw the rectangle using OpenGL.
Represents a rectangle object for 2D rendering using OpenGL.
2D Objects:

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Circle_2D

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Circle_2D.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Circle with x, y coordinates, radius, fill color,
and optional frame color and width.

```
param x

X-coordinate of the center.

param y

Y-coordinate of the center.

param radius

Radius of the circle.

param fill_color

Tuple representing RGBA color (0-1 range).

param frame_color

Tuple representing RGBA frame color (0-1 range), optional.

param frame_width

Width of the frame line, optional.

param segments

Number of segments to approximate the circle.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Circle_2D.draw
documentation: Draw the circle using OpenGL.
Represents a circle object for 2D rendering using OpenGL.

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Image_2D

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Image_2D.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Image with the image path, position, and optional dimensions.

```
param image_path

Path to the image file.

param x

X-coordinate of the top-left corner.

param y

Y-coordinate of the top-left corner.

param width

Width to scale the image to, optional.

param height

Height to scale the image to, optional.

param rotation

Rotate image within its rendering zone.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.Image_2D.draw
documentation: Draw the image as a textured quad using OpenGL.
Represents an image object for 2D rendering using OpenGL.

inner_classes.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.SoundManager

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.SoundManager.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Pygame mixer and creates dictionaries to store sounds, their playing status, and whether they should loop.
\:param channel_amount: Set how many PyGame audio channels to create, default 8.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.SoundManager.add_sound_to_memory
documentation:
Adds a new sound to the manager.

```
param name

The name of the sound, string. Any you want.

param file_path

The file path of the sound file, use whatever format PyGame and your system support.

param loop

Whether the sound should loop (for example background music) or not (for example sound effect), bool.
```

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.SoundManager.play_sound
documentation:
Plays the specified sound.
\:param name: The name of the sound to play.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.SoundManager.stop_sound
documentation:
Stops the specified sound.
:param name (str): The name of the sound to stop.

methods.name: RenderBirdCore.RenderBirdCore.SoundManager.update
documentation:
Updates the playing status of the sounds.
Checks if any of the sounds that are currently playing have finished playing, and if so, sets their playing status to False.

Allows to use and control audio in your program.
Currently, it's just primitive audio playback, without support for audio within 3D space.
